steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference in umami intensity between two seaweeds: winged kelp (moderate umami) and arame (high umami). The correct choice must contrast the two levels, making clear that arame's umami is stronger.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Some types of brown seaweed, like winged kelp and arame, trigger umami flavor in human taste buds.: Does not clearly establish that arame's umami is greater than winged kelp's. ✗
  • Choice B — Although winged kelp and arame are types of brown seaweed, the latter's umami flavor is more intense.: Directly contrasts the two seaweeds by stating that while winged kelp has moderate umami, arame's is more intense — precisely the difference the student wants to highlight. ✓
  • Choice C — Winged kelp is a type of brown seaweed, but so is arame.: Does not frame the comparison as a contrast between the two intensities. ✗
  • Choice D — While winged kelp and arame contain umami flavor, umami can also be found in ham and mushrooms.: Does not emphasize that arame exceeds winged kelp in umami strength. ✗
That is the correct answer.
